assets.prompt: A geometric cross-section of filing cabinet drawers suspended in darkness, each drawer pulled open at different depths revealing layered translucent sheets instead of folders — some sheets crisp and aligned, others chaotic and overlapping. Soft directional light from upper left casts subtle shadows between the layers. Matte charcoal background fades to black at edges. Ochre accent on a single drawer pull. The composition suggests forensic archaeology: excavating meaning from accumulated strata, where legibility depends on knowing which layer to read first. No text, no people, no decorative elements — just structural metaphor for invisible translation work becoming visible under external scrutiny.

altText.en: Stacked cardboard archive boxes labeled with handwritten markers sit on metal shelving in a windowless storage room
altText.es: Cajas de archivo apiladas con etiquetas manuscritas sobre estantería metálica en una sala de almacenamiento sin ventanas

caption.en: Your books close on time, but two days vanish before analysis can even begin.
caption.es: Tus libros cierran a tiempo, pero dos días se evaporan antes de que el análisis siquiera comience.
